qa_pairs.question: What is the Canadian Common CV (CCV) system?
qa_pairs.answer: The Canadian Common CV (CCV) is a software system funded by the Canadian Institutes of Health Research (CIHR) that aims to standardize CV submissions to granting agencies for funding competitions.

qa_pairs.question: Why does the Alliance require faculty users to submit a Canadian Common CV (CCV)?
qa_pairs.answer: The Alliance requires all faculty users to submit a CCV to renew their Alliance account and to apply for the Resource Allocation Competitions (RAC).

qa_pairs.question: Which types of Alliance users are optionally required to submit a Canadian Common CV (CCV) for account renewal?
qa_pairs.answer: Emeritus Professors, Librarians, and Retired Faculty have the option not to submit a CCV when renewing their Alliance account.

qa_pairs.question: Who should be contacted for technical support if an Alliance account holder forgets their Canadian Common CV (CCV) username or password?
qa_pairs.answer: Alliance account holders needing technical support for CCV access or forgotten login credentials should contact the CCV helpdesk directly by email at support@cihr-irsc.gc.ca or by phone at 613-954-1968 or 1-888-603-4178.

qa_pairs.question: What contact information should be used for questions specific to the information required in the Alliance Canadian Common CV (CCV) template or issues submitting it to the Alliance?
qa_pairs.answer: For specific questions about the information required in the Alliance CCV template or issues submitting it to the Alliance, contact support@tech.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.question: What are the mandatory steps to register for a new Canadian Common CV (CCV) account?
qa_pairs.answer: To register for a new CCV account, go to ccv-cvc.ca, select your preferred language, click 'Login', then 'Register', fill out the form with a valid email, set your password and security questions, complete the 'I'm not a robot' verification, and click 'Submit'.

qa_pairs.question: What is the specific "Funding Source" and "CV Type" that must be selected when submitting a Canadian Common CV (CCV) to the Alliance?
qa_pairs.answer: When submitting a CCV to the Alliance, the 'Funding Source' must be 'Digital Research Alliance of Canada' and the 'CV Type' must be 'Alliance'.

qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of the confirmation number provided after successfully submitting an Alliance Canadian Common CV (CCV)?
qa_pairs.answer: The confirmation number assigned upon submission of an Alliance CCV is mandatory to enter on the CCDB to link a Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application with the CCV submission.

qa_pairs.question: Can a confirmation number from a NSERC or CIHR Canadian Common CV (CCV) template be used for Alliance submissions?
qa_pairs.answer: No, only confirmation numbers for CCVs submitted using the Alliance template will be accepted; confirmation numbers from other CCV templates (e.g., NSERC, CIHR) cannot be used.

qa_pairs.question: What are the mandatory sections that must be completed in the Alliance Canadian Common CV (CCV) template?
qa_pairs.answer: The mandatory sections for the Alliance CCV template are Personal Information, Education, Employment, and Research Funding History over the last five years.

qa_pairs.question: What is the validity period for a Canadian Common CV (CCV) when renewing an Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: To renew an Alliance account, the Canadian Common CV (CCV) must have been submitted within the last 300 days.

qa_pairs.question: What is the validity period for a Co-Principal Investigator's Canadian Common CV (CCV) when applying for a Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) with the Alliance?
qa_pairs.answer: For Co-Principal Investigators of a Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application, their Canadian Common CV (CCV) must have been submitted within the last 90 days.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if an Academic Principal Investigator (PI) of a Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application has a Canadian Common CV (CCV) on file older than 90 days?
qa_pairs.answer: If an Academic Principal Investigator's (PI) Canadian Common CV (CCV) on file is older than 90 days, they will be required to submit a new CCV with their Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application.

qa_pairs.question: When an Academic Principal Investigator (PI) uploads a new Canadian Common CV (CCV), how are publications for which an answer has not yet been provided identified in the CCV Publications Reporting section?
qa_pairs.answer: When a new CCV is uploaded, publications for which an answer has not yet been provided are identified by a red border in the CCV Publications Reporting section of the CCV submission form on CCDB.

qa_pairs.question: How does an Academic Principal Investigator (PI) indicate that a publication was *not* enabled by Alliance computational resources in the Canadian Common CV (CCV) Publications Reporting section?
qa_pairs.answer: To mark a publication as not enabled by Alliance computational resources, an Academic PI must click twice on the publication's checkbox; the first click indicates it was enabled, and the second click removes the check mark, indicating it was not enabled.

qa_pairs.question: What should an Academic Principal Investigator (PI) do if they add new publications to their Alliance Canadian Common CV (CCV) after submitting an initial RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: If an Academic PI adds new publications, they should go to the CCV website, make the changes to the Alliance CCV template, submit it, enter the new confirmation code on CCDB, and then address only the newly added publications (those with a red border) in the CCV Publications Reporting section.

qa_pairs.question: What are the two ways a Co-Principal Investigator (Co-PI) can update their Canadian Common CV (CCV) for a Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application?
qa_pairs.answer: Co-Principal Investigators can update their CCV either by clicking 'Update CCV' under the 'Ongoing Competitions' tab in the Resource Applications menu, or by clicking 'View reporting' in the 'My Account' menu.

qa_pairs.question: Who is eligible for Alliance support regarding Canadian Common CV (CCV) submissions?
qa_pairs.answer: Alliance provides support only for Alliance account holders who need to submit a CCV to renew an account or apply for a Resource Allocation Competition (RAC).
